name: Lafayette Consolidated Government Neighborhood Counseling Services
description_short: Lafayette Consolidated Government's Housing Counseling Services offers FREE homeownership training, financial literacy workshops, and home maintenance education to the public.
description_long:
Lafayette Consolidated Government (LCG) is the municipal and parish government entity serving Lafayette, Louisiana. Housing Counseling Services operates under LCG's community development and planning division, providing no-cost financial and housing education to residents. The service is part of the Taylor Center 2026 Education Schedule and focuses on helping the public build foundational knowledge around home ownership and personal finance. This is a government-operated program, not a private non-profit or credit counseling agency, making it a direct public resource funded by municipal operations.

Housing Counseling Services offers three primary FREE programs: Home Ownership Training for prospective buyers, Financial Literacy Workshops covering money management fundamentals, and Home Maintenance Workshops teaching property upkeep. All services require pre-registration by phone. The program is designed for residents at any stage of the homeownership journey—from renters considering purchase to current homeowners seeking maintenance knowledge. There is no indication of credit counseling, debt management, or credit repair services on the website.

As a government agency offering free services, Lafayette Housing Counseling Services distinguishes itself through zero cost to participants and direct public accountability. The program appears to focus on preventative financial education rather than crisis intervention or remediation. The requirement for pre-registration suggests limited capacity and a structured, small-group approach. This is not a HUD-approved housing counselor program or NFCC-certified credit counseling agency based on available information.

The primary limitation is the absence of evidence regarding counselor credentials, HUD approval status, or NFCC certification on the public website. No contact information, specific program dates, or detailed curriculum descriptions are provided beyond the announcement. The program appears to be in early promotion phase for 2026 offerings. Consumers seeking specialized credit counseling or debt management should verify whether this service extends beyond basic financial literacy education.
pros: Completely FREE—no fees or hidden costs for any program
Government-operated with direct public accountability and transparency
Three distinct program tracks: home ownership, financial literacy, and maintenance
Pre-registration system suggests personalized, small-group instruction
Part of official municipal Taylor Center education schedule with consistent offering
No commercial motive—pure public service mission
cons: No visible credentials listed—HUD approval status or NFCC certification not documented on website
Pre-registration required; limited availability and potentially long waitlists
Minimal program details provided; no curriculum, instructor bios, or success metrics published
No evidence of credit counseling or debt management services beyond basic financial literacy
Contact information and scheduling details buried in general government website, not dedicated program page
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Lafayette Parish seeking foundational purchasing knowledge
Renters considering homeownership who need basic financial readiness education
Current homeowners wanting to learn preventative property maintenance and upkeep
diagnosis: Best for Lafayette Parish residents seeking free introductory financial education and homeownership preparation with no commercial strings attached. Primary caveat: This is a basic financial literacy and housing education program, not a full-service credit counseling or HUD-approved housing counselor agency—verify that the scope matches your specific needs before enrolling.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
